design_notes: Harder than iter-1: each eval has a trap (group-locking no-op; named vs request tranche; ProcArrayLock+syscache ordering). Assertions go beyond cite-the-line-number and test whether the answer identifies the real failure mode.

evals.trap: User proposes heavyweight advisory lock for leader/worker serialization. The trap is that heavyweight locks within a lock group don't conflict.

assertions.text: Explicitly states heavyweight/advisory lock is wrong because lock-group members don't conflict
Cites lock.c around 1610-1618 (LockCheckConflicts subtracts same-group holders) with line numbers
Names RELATION_EXTEND as the one exception that still conflicts within a group, with file:line
Cites README:629-634 (or near) for the conceptual statement of group-no-conflict
Recommends an LWLock (named tranche, RequestNamedLWLockTranche, or DSM-resident via LWLockNewTrancheId/Register) as the replacement
Mentions that LWLockAcquire has no lock-group semantics (it serializes raw waiters)
Recommends a code comment defending the LWLock choice so a future patch doesn't 'cleanup' to advisory lock

evals.trap: Lists files but misses the 'add at end / don't renumber' rule, or conflates PG_LWLOCK with RequestNamedLWLockTranche.

assertions.text: Names BOTH lwlocklist.h and wait_event_names.txt as required edits
States the 'add at end, don't renumber' rule and explains why (DTrace / external scripts)
Cites the lwlocklist.h header comment (lines ~22-32) for that rule
Identifies the next free ID (58) or refers to 'one past the current last entry' explicitly
Notes that names omit the 'Lock' suffix in the macro (added by codegen)
Correctly distinguishes PG_LWLOCK (core, fixed ID, must edit .txt) vs RequestNamedLWLockTranche (extensions, dynamic, no .txt edit)
Mentions the third pattern (LWLockNewTrancheId + LWLockInitialize + LWLockRegisterTranche) for DSM-resident locks
Recommends README update in the subsystem owning the lock

evals.trap: Surface reading says 'LWLock auto-releases on error, so this is fine'. The real bug is lock ordering / hold time / uncancellable wait.

assertions.text: Rejects the 'LWLock is error-safe' justification as a non-sequitur / insufficient
Identifies that SearchSysCache1 takes a heavyweight lock on pg_authid (AccessShareLock) on miss
Identifies that SearchSysCache1 takes BufMapping partition LWLock + buffer content LWLocks
Names the resulting lock-order claim explicitly (ProcArrayLock -> heavyweight -> BufMapping -> content_lock) and flags it as the real bug
States LWLocks have no deadlock detector (silent hang) as the consequence of the ordering violation
Cites README:39-45 for query-cancel-held-off-during-LWLock-wait
Explains LW_EXCLUSIVE makes contention worse but the bug class is identical
Correctly says MAX_SIMUL_LWLOCKS=200 is not the real concern here (only a few extra transient holds)
Recommends concrete fix: hoist syscache lookup out of ProcArrayLock section (read fields under lock, release, lookup, optionally re-acquire briefly)
